```json
[
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Network Density Surge Bullish Signal",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap API daily charger counts by region",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Rapid weekly increases in public EV charger deployment signal infrastructure capex momentum and green energy transition acceleration, which historically precedes XLK and XLI strength.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "EV charger buildout drives tech hardware demand and signals bullish macro sentiment on energy transition."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly EV charger count growth exceeds 2.5% in any US region and total count crosses prior 52-week percentile 75th, go long XLK.",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 1%.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Infrastructure expansion is ongoing; charger networks are actively scaling in major US metros."
  },
  {
    "idea": "FRED Commercial Paper Stress Widening Bearish Fade",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series AA-rated vs. Prime commercial paper spread (MMNRNJ vs MMNRJD)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Widening spreads between AA-rated and Prime commercial paper signal corporate credit stress and rising default fear, leading to flight-to-safety and sector rotation away from cyclicals.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Commercial paper stress is a leading indicator of credit-sensitive equity weakness, especially consumer discretionary."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If CP spread widens by more than 15 bps in one week and remains elevated, short XLY.",
      "exit": "After 12 trading days or if spread tightens by 10 bps.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Credit market volatility is frequent; CP spreads fluctuate weekly and signal genuine stress episodes regularly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Regional Extreme Weather Google Trend Spike Insurance Play",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly searches for 'flooding', 'hurricane prep', 'power outage' + Open-Meteo daily extreme weather events",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true

      "direction": "bullish",

      "entry": "If Google Trends weekly search interest for weather disaster keywords exceeds prior 52-week median by 50% AND Open-Meteo detects severe weather in 3+ US states, go long XLF.",

      "expected_frequency": "medium"

    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",

  },
  {
    "idea": "FRED Yield Curve Inversion Reversion Momentum Snap",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED 10-year vs 2-year yield spread (T10Y2Y)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When the yield curve inverts (2yr > 10yr) for 3+ weeks then suddenly re-steepens by 15+ bps in one week, it signals a capitulation reversal and flight-to-risk rally.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Curve un-inversion after sustained inversion triggers risk-on rotation; consumer discretionary leads the rebound."

    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If curve has been inverted for 20+ days and spreads 20+ bps steeper in one week, long XLY.",

    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Curve inversions and reversals occur in monetary cycles; steepening bounces fire multiple times per year."
  },
  {

    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Load factor collapse and recovery cycle creates near-term airline margin tailwinds and travel discretionary strength."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly load factor drops 3+ pts vs prior week and is below 52-week median, long XLY.",

    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Airline capacity and demand cycles produce load factor swings monthly; recovery bounces are reliable within 2-week windows."
  },
  {

    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Jobless claims spike frequently; threshold fires 3-5 times per quarter as labor volatility is persistent."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Oil Price Momentum Reversal Energy Sector Bounce",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",

    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Oil bounce signals reversal of supply shock fears; energy equities repricing higher on macro stabilization."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If crude down 3+ consecutive days, then up 2%+ next day, go long XLE.",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days or if crude falls 1.5%+.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Oil intraday and multi-day swings are frequent; 3-day losing streaks followed by bounces occur multiple times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Real Estate Mortgage Rate Google Trends Capitulation Signal",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly searches for 'mortgage rates', 'home refinance', 'housing affordability' + FRED mortgage rates (MORTGAGE30US)",

      "stable": true

    "behavior": "When Google Trends searches for mortgage/refinance spike 40%+ above 52-week median AND mortgage rates have fallen 30+ bps in prior week, it signals capitulation by underwater borrowers; XLRE rebounds.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Rate drops and search capitulation signal housing demand inflection; real estate equities rerate higher."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If mortgage Google Trends spike >40% above median AND rates dropped 30+ bps prior week, long XLRE.",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or if rates rise 15+ bps.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Housing cycles and rate swings produce search spikes monthly; conditions align 2-4 times per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Rail Carload Collapse Logistics Sector Fade",
    "family": "freight_logistics",

      "entry": "If carloads down 5%+ YoY and below 52-week median, short XLI.",

    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",

    "minimum_history_months": 24,

    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Rail carload volatility and YoY declines occur in economic slowdowns; threshold fires 2-3 times per year reliably."

  {
    "idea": "Healthcare Cost Sentiment RSS News Volume Spike Volatility Play",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",

      "source": "RSS feed count daily aggregation for 'healthcare cost', 'drug price', 'insurance premium' from major news sources",

      "direction": "bearish",

    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily healthcare cost RSS count spikes 60%+ above 30-day avg, short XLV or buy XLV puts.",

      "expected_frequency": "high"

    "expected_fire_rate": "daily",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
```
